blockingIssues.description: ASCII/Hermes terminology and ASCII-specific negative assertions still remain across component and app visual test suites, so the rerun did not complete the required terminology purge to the approved single-shader-plus-film-grain framing.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/design-system.md` to remove stale green-glow guidance and clarify that `#00ff00` is status-signal-only with no glow utilities or green fills.
Updated `.factory/library/tradebetter-exact-design-reference.md` to tighten the approved film-grain wording to a vendored animated GIF and to note that BETTER does not carry over a green glow token.

Updated `.factory/library/tradebetter-comparison-evidence.md` to remove stale blue-border/CTA implications and old ASCII or scanline comparison notes so the shared reference now matches the approved shader-plus-film-grain stack and monochrome UI rules.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Only allow `followedProcedure=true` when the transcript shows the required library/reference reads, failing-test-first evidence when applicable, and browser validation for rendered user-facing changes; otherwise require an explicit deviation entry.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: All four rerun reviews noted at least one mismatch between claimed skill adherence and transcript evidence (missing library reads, missing failing-test-first evidence, or missing/incomplete browser validation) while handoffs still marked `followedProcedure=true`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When a cleanup feature says to remove deprecated terminology or references, state explicitly that the purge includes negative assertions/comments in both component-level and app-level test suites, not only shipped runtime code.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The remaining blocking failure in `redesign-purge-ascii-test-terminology-cleanup` came from stale ASCII/Hermes wording and negative assertions surviving in `src/components/visual/__tests__` and `src/app/__tests__` after the worker only replaced one headline test file.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Reconcile the liquid-metal card blur rule across mission guidance, feature briefs, and the shared `LiquidMetalCard` implementation so workers are not told both to use blur and to avoid backdrop blur.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The passing card-migration review still recorded that mission AGENTS and library docs say `No backdrop-blur` while `src/components/LiquidMetalCard.tsx` continues to hard-code `blur(10px)`, causing recurring review noise across visual card features.
